signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 159.74 — 300bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains bid; Middle East tensions and SNB's Schlegel's warning on global uncertainty introduce intervention risk on a break above 160.00. JPY COT LF short −65,309 (crowded carry). Carry dominates until VIX > 25; 160.00 is the key intervention threshold.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 vs HV30 at 8.2% and ATM IV at 9.4% — options pricing a 1.2 vol-pt premium over realized. 25d RR at −0.14: mild put skew despite the bullish price trend. Market hedging rather than chasing — late-trend caution signal. A LF delta turning positive would invalidate the caution read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Carry
signals.text: CAD leads G8 (+0.463% avg, 3 pairs) while EUR lags (−0.515% avg, 7 pairs); G8 spread: 0.978%. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% — 175bp carry advantage drives the divergence. EUR underperformance despite higher ECB rates suggests real-money or CB repricing is overriding carry trade.

signals.title: GBP/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — 0bp GBP carry neutral. GBP LF net long +28,426; AM constructive — positioning aligned with carry read. ATM IV 9.5% near HV, 25d RR flat — options neutral. GBP/USD at 1.3482 mid-range; 1.3600 is the next resistance on a continuation.
